meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force Awards $90 Million to Rocket Lab for Two GEO Satellites
immediate.summary: Space Force has awarded a $90 million contract to Rocket Lab for satellites to host payloads that tracks objects in geosynchronous orbit. The post Space Force Awards $90 Million to Rocket Lab for Two GEO Satellites appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
immediate.summary: Rocket Lab has secured its first orders for two Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites from the U.S. Space Force for a space domain awareness (SDA) mission. Rocket Lab announced a $90 million [] The post Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
immediate.summary: Media are invited to NASA’s Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia on Wednesday, June 17, to view Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket, carrying a Katalyst robotic spacecraft that will attempt to boost the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. Katalyst’s robotic servicing spacecraft, called LINK, will launch on Pegasus in June to rendezvous with Swift []

immediate.title: KC-135 Pulled Out of Boneyard Weeks After Another Crashed in Iraq
immediate.summary: The Air Force pulled a KC-135 out of storage at the “Boneyard” in early April, three weeks after another one of the service’s Stratotankers crashed in Iraq during Operation Epic Fury. The post KC-135 Pulled Out of Boneyard Weeks After Another Crashed in Iraq appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: US military test-launches nuclear-capable ICBM from California (photos)
immediate.summary: The U.S. military test-launched an unarmed intercontinental ballistic missile from Vandenberg Space Force Base on May 20, 2026.

immediate.title: The Pentagon’s $54 billion bet on autonomous warfare
immediate.summary: With new DAWG initiative, the DOD is attempting to fix a historically slow-moving, broken acquisition pipeline.

immediate.title: This cryptocurrency billionaire will fly SpaceX's 1st private Starship to Mars, but when?
immediate.summary: SpaceX says it will launch a private flyby mission to Mars that will be led by cryptocurrency billionaire Chun Wang, but when it will fly is unclear.

immediate.title: A-10 Revamped: Warthog Flying in Middle East with New Refueling Probe, EW Pod
immediate.summary: A-10 Thunderbolt II attack planes in the Middle East are flying with fresh modifications as the Air Force looks to make the plane more versatile amid America’s ongoing blockade of Iranian ports and a tenuous ceasefire in the U.S. air war against Iran. The post A-10 Revamped: Warthog Flying in Middle East with New Refueling Probe, EW Pod appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Wilsbach: No Gap in USAF’s Close Air Support After A-10s Retire
immediate.summary: Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Kenneth S. Wilsbach told lawmakers this week there will be no gap in the service’s close air support capabilities under its latest plan to retire the venerable A-10 Thunderbolt II at the start of the next decade. The post Wilsbach: No Gap in USAF’s Close Air Support After A-10s Retire appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
immediate.summary: NASA will provide live coverage on Wednesday, May 27, as two Roscosmos cosmonauts conduct a spacewalk outside the International Space Station. The spacewalk is scheduled to begin at approximately 10:15 a.m. EDT and last roughly five hours. Watch NASA’s live coverage beginning at 9:45 a.m. on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and the agency’s YouTube channel. Learn []

immediate.title: SpaceX's 1st Starship V3 megarocket launch scrubbed at last minute. Sorry, Nicki Minaj
immediate.summary: SpaceX's new "V3" Starship megarocket was supposed to fly for the first time ever on Thursday (May 21), but technical issues nixed that plan.

immediate.title: Boots on the moon needed to beat ‘belligerent’ China: Mitchell Institute
immediate.summary: [F]uture American space security is at risk. Chinas military-led human space flight progression is positioning the Peoples Liberation Army to achieve strategic advantage in lunar access, infrastructure, and resources, Kyle Puma, Mitchell Institute senior resident fellow for space studies, said today.

immediate.title: YFQ-42A Collaborative Combat Aircraft Returns to Flight with Software Fix After Crash
immediate.summary: The YFQ-42A Collaborative Combat Aircraft has resumed flight testing after a six-week pause initiated when one of the drones crashed in early April. The post YFQ-42A Collaborative Combat Aircraft Returns to Flight with Software Fix After Crash appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Kadena to Get First F-15EXs in 2027 After Delay, Meink Says
immediate.summary: The first F-15EX Eagle IIs will arrive at Kadena Air Base in Okinawa, Japan, in 2027—a year later than originally planned—and the last of 36 planned fighters will likely get there in 2028, Air Force Secretary Troy Meink said May 21. The post Kadena to Get First F-15EXs in 2027 After Delay, Meink Says appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
immediate.summary: The Space Forces Space Systems Command (SSC) and the services SpaceWERX innovation arm are to launch an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to find and advance commercial technologies to sustain satellites. [] The post SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Space Force Sees Path to Operationalize On-Orbit Logistics Demos
immediate.summary: As the Space Force makes plans for two on-orbit refueling and maneuver demonstrations in 2027—and closely watches similar government and commercial endeavors—the service official overseeing the effort say he want to take these capabilities from demo to operations in short order. The post Space Force Sees Path to Operationalize On-Orbit Logistics Demos appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New Report: Time Is Now to Start Work on Guardians in Space to Counter China
immediate.summary: To counter Chinese ambitions, the U.S. Space Force must start work now to put Guardians in orbit and on the moon in the decades to come, according to a new paper from AFA’s Mitchell Institute for Aerospace Studies. The post New Report: Time Is Now to Start Work on Guardians in Space to Counter China appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Space debris is forcing satellites to dodge more often — costing us vital science. 'Things will get worse before they get better'
immediate.summary: Satellites forced to avoid space debris appear to have lost some of their data due to the movement. They've also lost precious fuel.

immediate.title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
immediate.summary: SpaceX generated $18.67B in revenue in 2025, according to paperwork filed today with the Securities and Exchange Commission ahead of the company’s IPO, expected next month. The post SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO appeared first on Payload .

M51 is one of four nearby galaxies observed by NASAs James Webb Space Telescope in a study of nearly 9,000 star clusters. Data from the study shows that more massive star clusters emerge more []

same_day.summary: Drones took the heaviest hit, accounting for 25 of the 42 aircraft losses listed in the report.

same_day.title: General Atomics CCA drone returns to flight
same_day.summary: Air Force Col. Timothy Helfrich said the response to the drone’s April 6 crash “validates our approach to accept acquisition/test risk instead of operational risk allowing us to accelerate the program towards fielding.”

same_day.title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
same_day.summary: A material recently discovered and tested at NASA’s Glenn Research Center in Cleveland could help astronauts pack lighter for future missions to the Moon. NASA is researching ways explorers could “live off the land” by harnessing lunar resources, including melting Moon rocks to extract metals for building infrastructure and oxygen for fuel and life support. []
